title: Saguaster propinquus
taxonomy: Saguaster propinquus is actually a synonym of Saguaro (Carnegiea gigantea) but I will provide information on the classification of the genus Saguaro, which is actually Carnegiea. uses: Saguaster propinquus, also known as Saguaro fruit, has been traditionally used by the Tohono O'odham people for its medicinal properties. The fruit and seeds of the plant are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which may help to boost the immune system and protect against various diseases. side_effects: Saguaster propinquus, also known as the desert prickly pear or Mexican prickly pear cactus, is a species of plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant's fleshy fruits, also known as tunas, are edible but can cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, in some individuals.
